title: Fix invoice due-date timezone bug
description: Fix a bug where invoice due-dates were computed in UTC instead of the customer's timezone, causing some invoices to appear a day late. Adds a regression test and a changelog entry.
diff:
diff --git a/billing/invoices.py b/billing/invoices.py
--- a/billing/invoices.py
+++ b/billing/invoices.py
@@
     net_days = terms.get("net_days", 30)
-    due = created + timedelta(days=net_days)
+    due = (created + timedelta(days=net_days)).astimezone(customer_tz)
     return due
diff --git a/tests/test_invoices.py b/tests/test_invoices.py
--- a/tests/test_invoices.py
+++ b/tests/test_invoices.py
@@
+def test_due_date_uses_customer_tz():
+    inv = make_invoice(net_days=30, customer_tz=ZoneInfo("America/Los_Angeles"))
+    assert inv.due.tzinfo is not None
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@
+## Unreleased
+- Fix invoice due-dates to respect the customer timezone.
diff --git a/package.json b/package.json
--- a/package.json
+++ b/package.json
@@
-  "version": "2.1.3",
+  "version": "2.1.4",
